2605.05463.title: Robustness of Graph Self-Supervised Learning to Real-World Noise: A Case Study on Text-Driven Biomedical Graphs
2605.05463.paper_summary: Graph Self-Supervised Learning (GSSL) offers a powerful paradigm for learning graph representations without labeled data. However, existing work assumes clean, manually curated graphs. Recent advances in NLP enable the large-scale automatic extraction of knowledge graphs from text, opening new opportunities for GSSL while introducing substantial real-world noise. This type of noise remains largely unexplored, as prior robustness studies typically rely on synthetic perturbations. To address this gap, we present the first comprehensive evaluation of GSSL methods on text-driven graphs for unsupervised term typing. We introduce Noise-Aware Text-Driven Graph GSSL (NATD-GSSL), a unified framework that combines automatic graph construction, graph refinement, and GSSL. Our evaluation follows a dual-graph protocol that contrasts a noisy graph derived from MedMentions with a clean Unified Medical Language System (UMLS) reference graph, aligned through a shared gold standard. Our results reveal variability in robustness across both pretext tasks and Graph Neural Network (GNN) architectures. Relation reconstruction is highly sensitive to noise and benefits from well-defined schemas, whereas feature reconstruction is considerably more robust, achieving performance comparable to clean-graph settings. Contrastive objectives are generally less affected by noise but depend strongly on alignment with downstream tasks. GNN architecture also plays a critical role: bidirectional relational message-passing designs are better suited to noisy, text-driven graphs, while unidirectional relational ones perform best on clean graphs. Overall, NATD-GSSL provides practical guidance for applying GSSL to real-world, noisy graphs and achieves up to a 7\% improvement over pretrained language model baselines. All code and benchmarks are publicly available at https://github.com/OthmaneKabal/MC2GAE.
2605.05463.paper_summary_zh: 圖形自監督學習（GSSL）提供了一種強大的範式，用於在沒有標記數據的情況下學習圖形表示。<lb/>然而，現有的研究假設圖形是乾淨且手動整理的。<lb/>近期在自然語言處理（NLP）方面的進展使得從文本中自動提取知識圖的規模化成為可能，這為GSSL開辟了新的機會，同時引入了大量的現實世界噪聲。<lb/>這種類型的噪聲仍然在很大程度上未被探索，因為之前的穩健性研究通常依賴於合成擾動。<lb/>為了解決這一空白，我們首次對基於文本的圖形進行了GSSL方法的綜合評估，以進行無監督的術語類型標記。<lb/>我們引入了噪聲感知文本驅動圖GSSL（NATD-GSSL），這是一個統一框架，結合了自動圖構建、圖精煉和GSSL。<lb/>我們的評估遵循雙圖協議，對比了從MedMentions衍生的噪聲圖與通過共享金標準對齊的乾淨統一醫學語言系統（UMLS）參考圖。<lb/>我們的結果顯示，在預訓練任務和圖神經網絡（GNN）架構之間的穩健性存在變異性。<lb/>關係重建對噪聲高度敏感，並從明確的模式中受益，而特徵重建則顯著更具穩健性，實現了與乾淨圖設置相當的性能。<lb/>對比目標通常不太受噪聲影響，但強烈依賴於與下游任務的對齊。<lb/>GNN架構也發揮了關鍵作用：雙向關係消息傳遞設計更適合於噪聲的文本驅動圖，而單向關係的設計在乾淨圖上表現最佳。<lb/>總體而言，NATD-GSSL為將GSSL應用於現實世界的噪聲圖提供了實用指導，並實現了比預訓練語言模型基準高出7%的改進。<lb/>所有代碼和基準測試均可在https://github.com/OthmaneKabal/MC2GAE上公開獲取。
2605.05463.author: Othmane Kabal et.al.
2605.05463.authors: Othmane Kabal, Mounira Harzallah, Fabrice Guillet, Hideaki Takeda, Ryutaro Ichise
